bug_description: Stack-use-after-scope in xmlEscapeText (xmlIO.c): xmlEscapeText uses data from a stack-allocated temporary buffer after that buffer's lifetime ends, causing a read of stale stack memory (observed via a memcpy). This is reachable via node string handling / element validation (xmlNodeListGetStringInternal -> xmlValidateElement) and can lead to crashes or other memory-corruption impacts when parsing crafted XML.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --postvalid /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout f0983199e821ef7a5fe57c7eeb7fb06dd9664138

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: A heap-buffer-overflow in libxml2's parser.c (xmlParsePubidLiteral) allows a one-byte out-of-bounds write when parsing PUBLIC identifiers (reachable via xmlParseExternalID/xmlParseDocTypeDecl). This out-of-bounds write can corrupt heap memory, causing crashes and potentially enabling arbitrary code execution when processing crafted DOCTYPE/external ID data.

bug_description: A heap-buffer-overflow in xmlParsePubidLiteral (parser.c): when parsing PublicID/external ID literals (via xmlParseExternalID/xmlParseEntityDecl), a buffer is resized/mis-sized leading to a one-byte write past the end of a heap allocation. This results in heap memory corruption that can cause crashes and may be exploitable for further attacks (e.g., arbitrary code execution).

bug_description: A heap-buffer-overflow in libxml2's parser.c: the function xmlParsePubidLiteral can write past the end of an allocated buffer when parsing a PUBLIC (pubid) literal. The overflow corrupts heap memory (write of one byte past the allocation) and is reachable via xmlParseExternalID/xmlParseNotationDecl during document parsing, potentially causing crashes or arbitrary code execution.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxslt.git libxslt
RUN git -C libxslt checkout c8b1ea4b89a9b81fa611f32c80f47df0c3b3b004
RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout 30665ae4d1a7de6e2f7db7c23042aedf4a9657c0
RUN git clone https://github.com/lsh123/xmlsec xmlsec
RUN git -C xmlsec checkout 94654ff9fb11bd376874eefead7f190efd0bc056

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap buffer overflow in xmlParsePubidLiteral (parser.c): when parsing PublicID literals (as part of external ID / entity declaration parsing) the parser can write one byte past the end of the allocated buffer, corrupting heap memory. A crafted XML input can trigger this, leading to crashes and potential exploitation via heap memory corruption.

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in parser.c (xmlParsePubidLiteral): while parsing a PUBLIC identifier, xmlParsePubidLiteral can write a byte past the end of an allocated heap buffer (seen via the xmlParseExternalID/xmlParseEntityDecl call path). This out‑of‑bounds heap write can corrupt memory, crash the process, or be exploited for arbitrary code execution or denial-of-service.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxslt.git libxslt
RUN git -C libxslt checkout c8b1ea4b89a9b81fa611f32c80f47df0c3b3b004
RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout 30665ae4d1a7de6e2f7db7c23042aedf4a9657c0

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in libxml2: xmlValidateRef (src/valid.c) can dereference an xmlAttr/xmlRef pointer that has already been freed when using the validating XML reader. The validator walks the document's reference table and may read stale pointers to attributes removed by the reader, leading to a heap-use-after-free that can cause crashes or other memory-corruption impacts.

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in xmlNodeDumpOutputInternal (xmlsave.c): during XInclude processing (xmlXIncludeIncludeNode in xinclude.c) namespace/node structures are freed (via xmlFreeNs/xmlFreeNode) while they are still reachable and later accessed by document-dumping functions (xmlDocContentDumpOutput / xmlDocDumpFormatMemoryEnc). This use-after-free can cause crashes and memory corruption, with potential for exploitation.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --noxincludenode --xinclude /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in xpath.c: xmlXPathNodeSetAdd (invoked via xmlXPathGetElementsByIds/xmlXPathIdFunction during XPath/XPointer evaluation) can access a node/attribute that was already freed while processing input (e.g. via xmlTextReader/XInclude). This results in reads of freed heap memory, leading to crashes and potential memory corruption when resolving ID-based references.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --stream --xinclude /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout f0fd1b67fc883a24cdd039abb3d4fe4696104d72

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in XInclude processing: xmlXIncludeLoadTxt (xinclude.c) can end up calling xmlStaticCopyNode (tree.c) on a node that has already been freed (by the XML reader code), resulting in a read of freed heap memory. An attacker-controlled XML with XInclude can trigger this, causing crashes, memory corruption, or potentially enabling information disclosure or further exploitation.

bug_description: A bad-cast/UBSan error in parser.c's xmlParseElement causes a null pointer dereference (read from a near-zero address) when handling certain inputs. The invalid cast to an xmlStartTag-like structure leads to a segmentation fault and undefined behavior during parsing, which can be triggered by crafted XML and results in a denial-of-service crash.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --sax --sax1 /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout ce00c36e654f0a5291d48bc437e807daecbd8449

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: An invalid-free in libxml2's parser (parser.c) causes xmlFreeNodeList/xmlFreeNode to attempt to free an address that was not obtained from malloc. bug_description: Invalid free and uninitialized-value in XInclude processing: while processing XInclude nodes (xmlXIncludeDoProcess in xinclude.c), libxml2's node-copy/free path (xmlStaticCopyNode/xmlFreeNode/xmlFreeNodeList/xmlFreeDoc in tree.c) attempts to free a pointer that was not returned by malloc (free on an interior address of an allocated block), causing heap corruption and crashes. MSAN also reports a use-of-uninitialized-value in the same XInclude/doc node creation path (xmlDictLookup/xmlNewDocNode), leading to undefined behavior and further memory corruption risk.

if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"

bug_description: A heap-use-after-free exists in xinclude.c: xmlXIncludeCopyXPointer reads memory that has already been freed during XInclude processing (called via xmlXIncludeIncludeNode/xmlXIncludeDoProcess). Malicious or malformed XInclude/XPointer input can trigger this read-after-free, leading to crashes or memory corruption and potential exploitation.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --xinclude --noxincludenode /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout e85f9b98a5389c69167176ae6600091e719ec38f

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: A global-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) occurs in xmlStrndup (xmlstring.c) when invoked from the HTML parser (HTMLparser.c) during DOCTYPE/system/public literal parsing (e.g., htmlParseSystemLiteral/htmlParseDocTypeDecl). The parser can supply an incorrect length/offset to xmlStrndup, causing it to read past the end of a buffer into adjacent global memory. This can crash the process and may lead to memory corruption or information disclosure.
build_sh:

    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"

secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --html /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:

    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git -C libxml2 checkout a6b9e55a9eb78e96f880afaf03ce8819bcd26a34

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: A global-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) occurs in xmlDictComputeFastKey / xmlDictLookup (dict.c) when handling NCName/QName parsing (xmlParseNCName / xmlParseQName in parser.c). The code reads one byte past a global string literal into adjacent global data (e.g. the xmlIsPubidChar_tab table), causing an out-of-bounds read that can crash the parser or expose unintended memory during XML parsing.

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in htmlCurrentChar (HTMLparser.c): an encoding name buffer is freed while still referenced by the HTML error reporting path (htmlParseErr / __xmlRaiseError), causing subsequent formatted error output to read freed heap memory. This can lead to crashes (denial of service) and may be exploitable for arbitrary code execution depending on context.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --html /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout b973ceaf2fa974897f6ac3ce287aeb07a0fad0fb

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in libxml2 parser: xmlCopyCharMultiByte (parserInternals.c), invoked from xmlParseCommentComplex in parser.c, can write one byte past the end of a dynamically allocated comment buffer while parsing XML comments. bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in parser.c (xmlParseCommentComplex): while parsing/assembling XML comments, the code can write past the end of an allocated comment buffer (one-byte overflow), corrupting heap memory. This can cause crashes and may be exploitable for arbitrary code execution when processing crafted XML input (observed via xmlParseComment/xmlParseMisc call path).
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxslt.git libxslt
RUN git -C libxslt checkout 42e018bdc9a91ba1f2898e33a3e86cf132c7e191
RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout 36374bc9fcf6e670dc9521ac032474066521858b

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in xmlCopyCharMultiByte (parserInternals.c), reachable from xmlParseCommentComplex/xmlParseComment (parser.c). While processing XML comments, multi-byte character copying can write past the end of a heap-allocated comment buffer due to insufficient allocation when accumulating comment text, leading to heap memory corruption, crashes, and potential arbitrary code execution when parsing crafted input.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxslt.git libxslt
RUN git -C libxslt checkout 42e018bdc9a91ba1f2898e33a3e86cf132c7e191
RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout 36374bc9fcf6e670dc9521ac032474066521858b

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in parser.c (xmlParseCommentComplex). While parsing XML comments the code can write past the end of a heap-allocated comment buffer (a 1-byte out-of-bounds write when accumulating/terminating comment text). This heap corruption, triggered via xmlParseComment/xmlParseMisc, can cause crashes or potentially be exploited for arbitrary code execution when processing crafted comments.

bug_description: A heap-use-after-free exists in libxml2's error reporting: xmlParserPrintFileContextInternal (error.c) can read from parser input pointers that reference freed heap memory when invoked via xmlReportError/__xmlRaiseError. This results in reads of deallocated memory during parsing (e.g., while handling entities or external subsets), leading to crashes or other undefined behavior and potentially enabling exploitation.
build_sh:
if [ "$SANITIZER" = undefined ]; then
    export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
    export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=unsigned-integer-overflow -fno-sanitize-recover=unsigned-integer-overflow"
fi
./autogen.sh     --disable-shared     --without-ftp     --without-http     --without-legacy     --without-python
make -j$(nproc) V=1
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --noent --loaddtd --maxmem 1000000 /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    case "$TARGETPLATFORM" in \
        'linux/arm64') EXTRA_PACKAGES='' ;; \
        *)             EXTRA_PACKAGES='zlib1g-dev:i386 liblzma-dev:i386' ;; \
    esac && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        make autoconf libtool pkg-config \
        zlib1g-dev liblzma-dev \
        $EXTRA_PACKAGES

# Build requires automake 1.16.3
RUN curl -LO http://mirrors.kernel.org/ubuntu/pool/main/a/automake-1.16/automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb && \
    apt install ./automake_1.16.5-1.3_all.deb

RUN git clone https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libxml2.git libxml2
RUN git -C libxml2 checkout 514ab399550a9bfcd4a5eb60e921d06a1d38af9e

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/libxml2
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/libxml2/xmllint --stream --noent /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/libxml2

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in libxml2's encoding cleanup: xmlCharEncCloseFunc (encoding.c) is invoked on an encoding handler that has already been freed during HTML output processing (htmlSaveFileFormat in HTMLtree.c / xmlOutputBufferClose in xmlIO.c). This leads to reads from freed heap memory, causing crashes or memory corruption and presenting a potential security risk when processing crafted HTML/XML input (e.g., via xmllint).

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in Mat_VarPrint (src/mat.c): when printing sparse matrix variables the function's inner loop can index past the allocated sparse arrays, performing out-of-bounds 4-byte reads from heap memory. The insufficient bounds checking on the sparse data/indices can lead to crashes, memory corruption, or other undefined behavior.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
# Check if autogen.sh exists and is executable
if [ -f ./autogen.sh ]; then
    chmod +x ./autogen.sh
    ./autogen.sh
fi

# Clean the project before building
make clean || true

# Set compiler flags
export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=address"
export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=address"

# Configure and build
./configure
make -j$(nproc)

build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
# Check if autogen.sh exists and is executable
if [ -f ./autogen.sh ]; then
    chmod +x ./autogen.sh
    ./autogen.sh
fi

# Clean the project before building

# Set compiler flags
export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fsanitize=address"
export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fsanitize=address"

# Configure and build
./configure
make -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/matio/tools/matdump /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/matio

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

This out-of-bounds access can corrupt stack memory when processing malformed or malicious MAT files, leading to crashes or other memory-corruption impacts (e.g., information disclosure or potential code execution).

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in src/range.c (range_num_to_a): while converting numeric ranges to an array the function can write an 8-byte value past the end of the allocated array (out-of-bounds write). This corrupts heap memory and can lead to crashes or potential arbitrary code execution when handling certain numeric ranges (e.g., mixes of integers and floats).
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -e

# Navigate to the mruby source directory
cd /src/mruby

# Set the linker to clang
export LD=clang

# Set linker flags
export LDFLAGS="-w -Wno-yacc -Wno-incompatible-pointer-types -fsanitize=address -fsanitize-address-use-after-scope"

# Build the project using rake with multiple processors
rake -m -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/mruby/build/host/bin/mruby /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mruby

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: mrb_ary_shift_m in src/array.c fails to validate negative shift counts. A negative shift value can be used when constructing/indexing the array storage, leading to an out-of-bounds/invalid memory read (segmentation fault). This can be triggered via array shift operations with negative arguments, causing crashes and other memory-safety issues (denial-of-service).
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -e

# Navigate to the mruby source directory
cd /src/mruby

# Set the linker to clang
export LD=clang

# Set linker flags
export LDFLAGS="-w -Wno-yacc -Wno-incompatible-pointer-types -fsanitize=address -fsanitize-address-use-after-scope"

# Build the project using rake with multiple processors
rake -m -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/mruby/build/host/bin/mruby /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mruby

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: A heap-buffer-overflow occurs in mrb_ary_splice (src/array.c), where value_move (src/value_array.h) performs an out-of-bounds read (8 bytes past a heap-allocated array) during array splice/assignment (mrb_ary_aset). This can cause heap memory corruption, crashes, or potentially enable arbitrary code execution.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -e

# Navigate to the mruby source directory
cd /src/mruby

# Set the linker to clang
export LD=clang

# Set linker flags
export LDFLAGS="-w -Wno-yacc -Wno-incompatible-pointer-types -fsanitize=address -fsanitize-address-use-after-scope"

# Build the project using rake with multiple processors
rake -m -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/mruby/build/host/bin/mruby /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mruby

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y build-essential ruby bison ninja-build cmake zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblzma-dev
RUN git clone https://github.com/google/protobuf.git external.protobuf
RUN git -C external.protobuf checkout 909a0f36a10075c4b4bc70fdee2c7e32dd612a72
RUN git clone https://github.com/google/libprotobuf-mutator.git libprotobuf-mutator
RUN git -C libprotobuf-mutator checkout ffd86a32874e5c08a143019aad1aaf0907294c9f
RUN git clone https://github.com/bshastry/mruby_seeds.git mruby_seeds
RUN git -C mruby_seeds checkout 5a618aba8e4493eceff7a430cba7c848e6cbabfd
RUN git clone https://github.com/mruby/mruby mruby
RUN git -C mruby checkout cd0ffd6f1ca413f36b6239de7779ae7778e1a200
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/mruby
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in mrb_funcall_with_block (src/vm.c): the VM's method-call path fails to reserve enough stack space before invoking methods, allowing an 8-byte write during nested mrb_funcall/mrb_equal calls (observed in rational_eq) to overflow the heap-allocated stack. This can lead to heap memory corruption, crashes, or potential arbitrary code execution.

rake -m -j$(nproc)

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in src/vm.c (mrb_funcall_with_block): the VM writes 8 bytes past an allocated stack buffer when preparing/invoking a method (including arguments/blocks), causing heap memory corruption. This can lead to crashes, denial-of-service, or potentially arbitrary code execution.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -e

# Navigate to the mruby source directory
cd /src/mruby

# Set the linker to clang
export LD=clang

# Set linker flags
export LDFLAGS="-w -Wno-yacc -Wno-incompatible-pointer-types -fsanitize=address -fsanitize-address-use-after-scope"

# Build the project using rake with multiple processors
rake -m -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/mruby/build/host/bin/mruby /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mruby

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y build-essential ruby bison ninja-build cmake zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblzma-dev
RUN git clone https://github.com/google/protobuf.git external.protobuf
RUN git -C external.protobuf checkout 909a0f36a10075c4b4bc70fdee2c7e32dd612a72
RUN git clone https://github.com/google/libprotobuf-mutator.git libprotobuf-mutator
RUN git -C libprotobuf-mutator checkout ffd86a32874e5c08a143019aad1aaf0907294c9f
RUN git clone https://github.com/bshastry/mruby_seeds.git mruby_seeds
RUN git -C mruby_seeds checkout 5a618aba8e4493eceff7a430cba7c848e6cbabfd
RUN git clone https://github.com/mruby/mruby mruby
RUN git -C mruby checkout 6b3b1012ca480f1e930c4ddeeaebed55e5df4144
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/mruby
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in mruby's VM: mrb_funcall_with_block (src/vm.c) can write past the end of a VM stack allocation. The VM fails to reserve sufficient stack/heap space when preparing/performing a method/function call (mrb_funcall_* path), allowing an 8-byte write beyond the allocated buffer. This leads to heap corruption and can cause crashes or potentially enable arbitrary code execution.

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in mruby's VM stack handling: mrb_funcall_with_block (src/vm.c) / related stack copy logic can read from freed VM stack memory (observed during calls such as mrb_instance_new / mrb_make_exception). This use-after-free can cause interpreter crashes, undefined behavior or memory corruption and may be exploitable with crafted input.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -e

# Navigate to the mruby source directory
cd /src/mruby

# Set the linker to clang
export LD=clang

# Set linker flags
export LDFLAGS="-w -Wno-yacc -Wno-incompatible-pointer-types -fsanitize=address -fsanitize-address-use-after-scope"

# Build the project using rake with multiple processors
rake -m -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/mruby/build/host/bin/mruby /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mruby

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: A heap-buffer-overflow exists in mrbgems/mruby-bigint/core/bigint.c: the udiv routine (invoked via mpz_mdiv / mrb_bint_div) can read past the end of its allocated limb buffers during multi-precision division, causing an out-of-bounds heap read. bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in mruby-bigint: mpz_init_set_str (mrbgems/mruby-bigint/core/bigint.c), invoked via mrb_bint_new_str, can read from freed or otherwise invalid heap memory when initializing a BigInt from a string. This results in a crash (SEGV) and potential memory corruption, with possible denial-of-service or further exploitation.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -e

# Navigate to the mruby source directory
cd /src/mruby

# Set the linker to clang
export LD=clang

# Set linker flags
export LDFLAGS="-w -Wno-yacc -Wno-incompatible-pointer-types -fsanitize=address -fsanitize-address-use-after-scope"

# Build the project using rake with multiple processors
rake -m -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/mruby/build/host/bin/mruby /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mruby

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y build-essential ruby bison ninja-build cmake zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblzma-dev
RUN git clone https://github.com/google/protobuf.git external.protobuf
RUN git -C external.protobuf checkout 21027a27c4c2ec1000859ccbcfff46d83b16e1ed
RUN git clone https://github.com/google/libprotobuf-mutator.git libprotobuf-mutator
RUN git -C libprotobuf-mutator checkout a304ec48dcf15d942607032151f7e9ee504b5dcf
RUN git clone https://github.com/bshastry/mruby_seeds.git mruby_seeds
RUN git -C mruby_seeds checkout 5a618aba8e4493eceff7a430cba7c848e6cbabfd
RUN git clone https://github.com/mruby/mruby mruby
RUN git -C mruby checkout a64d2bfbe67ce0520cdd9bb72ac8b9194269927a
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/mruby
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: A null pointer dereference in the bigint comparison path: cmpnum (src/numeric.c) can end up invoking mrb_bint_cmp which calls mpz_cmp (mrbgems/mruby-bigint/core/bigint.c) with an invalid/non-bigint operand, causing mpz_cmp to dereference a NULL/invalid pointer and trigger a segmentation fault. This leads to crashes (denial of service) during numeric comparisons.

patch:
commit 960021e51989881c06c7c87b2661440c1fb2a784
Author: Yukihiro "Matz" Matsumoto <matz@ruby.or.jp>
Date:   Fri Jul 8 21:57:05 2022 +0900

    numeric.c (cmpnum): call mrb_bigint_cmp() if v1 is bigint, not v2.

diff --git a/src/numeric.c b/src/numeric.c
index 27a7cb8c3..7c8754e49 100644
--- a/src/numeric.c
+++ b/src/numeric.c
@@ -1801,7 +1801,7 @@ static mrb_int
 cmpnum(mrb_state *mrb, mrb_value v1, mrb_value v2)
 {
 #ifdef MRB_USE_BIGINT
-  if (mrb_bigint_p(v2)) {
+  if (mrb_bigint_p(v1)) {
     return mrb_bint_cmp(mrb, v1, v2);
   }
 #endif

bug_description: A heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) exists in mpz_get_int (mrbgems/mruby-bigint/core/bigint.c): the function can read past the allocated bigint limb array (e.g. accessing y->p[1] beyond the allocation). This can be triggered during bigint comparisons (mrb_bint_cmp / num_gt) and may lead to crashes or other undefined behavior.
