bug_description: A NULL/uninitialized-pointer dereference (segmentation fault) in urshift (mrbgems/mruby-bigint/core/bigint.c). urshift does not correctly handle zero-valued mpz_t operands and can read from an invalid/garbage limb pointer when performing right-shift operations (observed via mpz_div_2exp -> mrb_bint_rshift). This leads to a crash (invalid memory read) and can be triggered by crafted inputs, causing denial-of-service or other undefined behavior in mruby.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -e

# Navigate to the mruby source directory
cd /src/mruby

# Set the linker to clang
export LD=clang

# Set linker flags
export LDFLAGS="-w -Wno-yacc -Wno-incompatible-pointer-types -fsanitize=address -fsanitize-address-use-after-scope"

# Build the project using rake with multiple processors
rake -m -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/mruby/build/host/bin/mruby /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mruby

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y build-essential ruby bison ninja-build cmake zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblzma-dev
RUN git clone https://github.com/google/protobuf.git external.protobuf
RUN git -C external.protobuf checkout 21027a27c4c2ec1000859ccbcfff46d83b16e1ed
RUN git clone https://github.com/google/libprotobuf-mutator.git libprotobuf-mutator
RUN git -C libprotobuf-mutator checkout a304ec48dcf15d942607032151f7e9ee504b5dcf
RUN git clone https://github.com/bshastry/mruby_seeds.git mruby_seeds
RUN git -C mruby_seeds checkout 5a618aba8e4493eceff7a430cba7c848e6cbabfd
RUN git clone https://github.com/mruby/mruby mruby
RUN git -C mruby checkout 97b9bfc4fa2fb909cd1e4bcd41f9a0e3a45c0ddf
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/mruby
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: An index-out-of-bounds write in mruby's pack/unpack handling: malformed pack templates can cause unpack_hex / pack_unpack (mrbgems/mruby-pack/src/pack.c) to drive string creation with an incorrect size, leading to an out-of-bounds write in str_new -> str_init_embed (src/mruby/src/string.c). This can crash the process (SIGSEGV) and cause memory corruption, potentially enabling further exploitation.

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) in memsearch_swar (src/string.c): during substring search the code can invoke memcmp past the end of the target buffer, causing reads beyond the allocated heap. This issue is reachable via mrb_memsearch / mrb_str_index / str_convert_range / mrb_str_aref and can lead to crashes or potential heap memory disclosure.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -e

# Navigate to the mruby source directory
cd /src/mruby

# Set the linker to clang
export LD=clang

# Set linker flags
export LDFLAGS="-w -Wno-yacc -Wno-incompatible-pointer-types -fsanitize=address -fsanitize-address-use-after-scope"

# Build the project using rake with multiple processors
rake -m -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/mruby/build/host/bin/mruby /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mruby

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in the mruby VM (mrb_vm_exec in src/vm.c). During certain VM execution paths (involving cipush/mrb_ary_splat and nested mrb_vm_exec calls) an object is reallocated/freed (mrb_realloc/mrb_realloc_simple) while still referenced by the VM, and the VM later reads from that freed heap memory. This can cause crashes and undefined behavior, and may enable memory corruption or further exploitation with crafted inputs.

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in pdf_load_embedded_font (source/pdf/pdf-font.c): a buffer is accessed after being freed (trace includes fz_drop_buffer and pdf_load_font_descriptor), causing reads of freed heap memory. A crafted PDF can trigger crashes, memory corruption, or potentially enable further exploitation.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash

export CFLAGS="-O1 -g -fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer"
export LDFLAGS="-fsanitize=address"
cd /src/mupdf 
make OUT=/out/mupdf HAVE_GLUT=no -j"$(nproc)"
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    MSAN_OPTIONS=halt_on_error=1 /out/mupdf/mutool draw -o /dev/null /testcase/poc
    return 1
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mupdf

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y make libtool pkg-config liblcms2-dev autoconf automake libtool cmake
RUN git clone https://github.com/ArtifexSoftware/mupdf mupdf
RUN git -C mupdf checkout 27e2d170cbb5b5f93a619267c97100a1ee0dec73
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

RUN cd $SRC/mupdf && git submodule update --init --recursive

COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

WORKDIR $SRC/mupdf

 	unsigned char *data;

patch:
commit 74e920a40e0aea93f7cefca6e6bbff249d637b18
Author: Sebastian Rasmussen <sebras@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 13 07:11:01 2023 +0100

    Use the correct buffer size when parsing cmap.
    
    pdf_parse_bf_range_array() lexes a hex string, converts it
    into binary and passes it to pdf_map_one_to_many().
    
    To handle fuzzed files commit a6b0a8273f2eb15fd5924501b6ad03e30f2c8d0a
    introduced a limit of the binary to 256 bytes. But instead of passing the
    (possibly limited) string length to pdf_map_one_to_many() it passed the
    unlimited length. This meant that when pdf_map_one_to_many() from commit
    6b0a8273f2eb15fd5924501b6ad03e30f2c8d0a started to handle surrogate pairs
    of more than two characters it might advance past the end of the string
    causing the reported issue.
    
    Fixes oss-fuzz issue 55051.

diff --git a/source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c b/source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c
index 07881da5a..d2cba367e 100644
--- a/source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c
+++ b/source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c
@@ -243,7 +243,7 @@ pdf_parse_bf_range_array(fz_context *ctx, pdf_cmap *cmap, fz_stream *file, pdf_l
 			for (i = 0; i < len; i++)
 				dst[i] = pdf_code_from_string(&buf->scratch[i * 2], 2);
 
-			pdf_map_one_to_many(ctx, cmap, lo, dst, buf->len / 2);
+			pdf_map_one_to_many(ctx, cmap, lo, dst, i);
 		}
 
 		lo ++;

bug_description: A stack-based buffer overflow in the PDF CMap parsing code: pdf_parse_bf_range_array (source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c) can cause pdf_map_one_to_many to read past the end of a converted buffer when handling multi-character mappings. This out‑of‑bounds access can lead to crashes and memory corruption, and may be exploitable when processing crafted PDF CMap data.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash

export CFLAGS="-O1 -g -fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer"
export LDFLAGS="-fsanitize=address"
cd /src/mupdf 
make OUT=/out/mupdf HAVE_GLUT=no -j"$(nproc)"
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /out/mupdf/mutool draw -o /tmp/out.png /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/mupdf

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y make libtool pkg-config liblcms2-dev autoconf automake libtool cmake
RUN git clone https://github.com/ArtifexSoftware/mupdf mupdf
RUN git -C mupdf checkout 360a6e0772e48272a738a9d825ba554387892ff7
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

RUN cd $SRC/mupdf && git submodule update --init --recursive

COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

WORKDIR $SRC/mupdf
patch:
commit 74e920a40e0aea93f7cefca6e6bbff249d637b18
Author: Sebastian Rasmussen <sebras@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 13 07:11:01 2023 +0100

    Use the correct buffer size when parsing cmap.
    
    pdf_parse_bf_range_array() lexes a hex string, converts it
    into binary and passes it to pdf_map_one_to_many().
    
    To handle fuzzed files commit a6b0a8273f2eb15fd5924501b6ad03e30f2c8d0a
    introduced a limit of the binary to 256 bytes. But instead of passing the
    (possibly limited) string length to pdf_map_one_to_many() it passed the
    unlimited length. This meant that when pdf_map_one_to_many() from commit
    6b0a8273f2eb15fd5924501b6ad03e30f2c8d0a started to handle surrogate pairs
    of more than two characters it might advance past the end of the string
    causing the reported issue.
    
    Fixes oss-fuzz issue 55051.

diff --git a/source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c b/source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c
index 07881da5a..d2cba367e 100644
--- a/source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c
+++ b/source/pdf/pdf-cmap-parse.c
@@ -243,7 +243,7 @@ pdf_parse_bf_range_array(fz_context *ctx, pdf_cmap *cmap, fz_stream *file, pdf_l
 			for (i = 0; i < len; i++)
 				dst[i] = pdf_code_from_string(&buf->scratch[i * 2], 2);
 
-			pdf_map_one_to_many(ctx, cmap, lo, dst, buf->len / 2);
+			pdf_map_one_to_many(ctx, cmap, lo, dst, i);
 		}
 
 		lo ++;

bug_description: Stack-buffer-underflow in the BMP loader: bmp_read_palette (called from bmp_read_image / fz_load_bmp_subimage in source/fitz/load-bmp.c) miscomputes palette data bounds and writes before the start of a stack-allocated structure ('info'). A crafted BMP image can trigger stack memory corruption, leading to crashes or potential remote code execution.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash

export CFLAGS="-O1 -g -fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer"
export LDFLAGS="-fsanitize=address"
cd /src/mupdf 
make OUT=/out/mupdf HAVE_GLUT=no -j"$(nproc)"

    if /usr/local/bin/compile \

        exit 1
    fi
}

}

    fi

}

            ;;
        patch)

    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1

bug_description: Heap-based buffer overflow (out-of-bounds read) in the BMP decoder (load-bmp.c) within bmp_read_color_profile. patch:
commit 711e82dde434dfe8f36feca88c5c4b9d70ab87ce
Author: Sebastian Rasmussen <sebras@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Jul 17 01:08:27 2024 +0200

    Check nesting depth before pushing clip mark.
    
    Without this the nesting depth might step outside the layer/clip stack.

diff --git a/source/pdf/pdf-op-run.c b/source/pdf/pdf-op-run.c
index 44464f6e7..5369540ae 100644
--- a/source/pdf/pdf-op-run.c
+++ b/source/pdf/pdf-op-run.c
@@ -211,6 +211,8 @@ flush_begin_layer(fz_context *ctx, pdf_run_processor *proc)
 
 static void nest_layer_clip(fz_context *ctx, pdf_run_processor *proc)
 {
+	if (proc->nest_depth == nelem(proc->nest_mark))
+		fz_throw(ctx, FZ_ERROR_LIMIT, "layer/clip nesting too deep");
 	proc->nest_mark[proc->nest_depth++] = CLIP_MARK;
 }

bug_description: A use-after-free / invalid pointer dereference in njs: njs_regexp_prototype_symbol_split (src/njs/src/njs_regexp.c) invokes njs_string_prop (src/njs/src/njs_string.c) on an invalid or freed string value from RegExp execution results. njs_string_prop can dereference a bogus/high-address pointer, causing a segmentation fault and other memory-safety issues when performing RegExp.prototype[Symbol.split] operations.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -eu
# Check if autogen.sh exists and run it if it does
if [ -f ./autogen.sh ]; then
    chmod +x ./autogen.sh
    ./autogen.sh
fi

# Set compiler flags
export CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fno-use-cxa-atexit"
export CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fno-use-cxa-atexit"

# Configure the build
./configure

# Build the project
make -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/njs/build/njs /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/njs

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: A use-of-uninitialized/invalid-memory read in Array.prototype.join causes a crash: njs_array_prototype_join passes an invalid/uninitialized string value into the buffer append code, triggering a read from an invalid address inside njs_chb_append0 (njs_chb.c). This leads to a segmentation fault/undefined behavior when joining array elements and can be triggered by crafted inputs (resulting in crashes or potential memory corruption).

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in generic_unpack (src/lib/OpenEXRCore/unpack.c): while unpacking/decoding image data (via exr_decoding_run / readCoreScanlinePart), generic_unpack performs an out-of-bounds read of 4 bytes past a heap-allocated buffer. This insufficient bounds checking / incorrect buffer sizing can cause crashes, memory corruption and may enable information disclosure or further exploitation when parsing crafted OpenEXR files.
build_sh:
cd $WORK/
CMAKE_SETTINGS=(
  "-D BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF"         # Build static libraries only
  "-D BUILD_TESTING=OFF"             # Or tests
  "-D OPENEXR_INSTALL_EXAMPLES=OFF"  # Or examples
  "-D OPENEXR_LIB_SUFFIX="           # Don't append the version number to library files
)
cmake $SRC/openexr ${CMAKE_SETTINGS[@]}
make -j$(nproc)
INCLUDES=(
  "-I $SRC"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXRCore"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXR"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXRUtil"
  "-I $WORK/cmake"
)
LIBS=(
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXRUtil/libOpenEXRUtil.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXR/libOpenEXR.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXRCore/libOpenEXRCore.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/IlmThread/libIlmThread.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/Iex/libIex.a"
  "$WORK/_deps/imath-build/src/Imath/libImath*.a"
)
for fuzzer in $SRC/openexr/src/test/OpenEXRFuzzTest/oss-fuzz/*_fuzzer.cc; do
  fuzzer_basename=$(basename -s .cc $fuzzer)
  $CXX $CXXFLAGS -std=c++11 -pthread ${INCLUDES[@]} $fuzzer $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE ${LIBS[@]} -lz     -o $OUT/$fuzzer_basename
done

secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /work/bin/exrcheck -m -t -s -c /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/openexr

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: A heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) occurs in src/lib/OpenEXRCore/internal_huf.c: the fasthuf_read_bits routine (invoked from fasthuf_initialize during Huffman table parsing and used by internal_huf_decompress / internal_exr_undo_piz) can read past the end of the input buffer when processing malformed or truncated Huffman table data. This can cause crashes or potential information disclosure during decompression.

bug_description: A heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) occurs during DWA decompression: while parsing compressed DWA blocks in DwaCompressor_uncompress (internal_dwa_compressor.h) via exr_uncompress_buffer and libdeflate_zlib_decompress_ex, a 16-bit unaligned read (get_unaligned_be16/load_u16_unaligned) can read two bytes past the end of the input buffer for malformed compressed data. This can crash the process (DoS) and may lead to information disclosure or further memory corruption when processing crafted EXR files.
build_sh:
cd $WORK/
CMAKE_SETTINGS=(
  "-D BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF"         # Build static libraries only
  "-D BUILD_TESTING=OFF"             # Or tests
  "-D OPENEXR_INSTALL_EXAMPLES=OFF"  # Or examples
  "-D OPENEXR_LIB_SUFFIX="           # Don't append the version number to library files
)
cmake $SRC/openexr ${CMAKE_SETTINGS[@]}
make -j$(nproc)
INCLUDES=(
  "-I $SRC"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXRCore"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXR"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXRUtil"
  "-I $WORK/cmake"
)
LIBS=(
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXRUtil/libOpenEXRUtil.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXR/libOpenEXR.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXRCore/libOpenEXRCore.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/IlmThread/libIlmThread.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/Iex/libIex.a"
  "$WORK/_deps/imath-build/src/Imath/libImath*.a"
)
for fuzzer in $SRC/openexr/src/test/OpenEXRFuzzTest/oss-fuzz/*_fuzzer.cc; do
  fuzzer_basename=$(basename -s .cc $fuzzer)
  $CXX $CXXFLAGS -std=c++11 -pthread ${INCLUDES[@]} $fuzzer $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE ${LIBS[@]} -lz     -o $OUT/$fuzzer_basename
done

secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /work/bin/exrcheck -c /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/openexr

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y make autoconf automake libtool mercurial git libpcre2-dev zlib1g-dev
RUN git clone https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/openexr openexr
RUN git -C openexr checkout 379a2884afe9b6d81060ffbd182c8021630a55e2
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/openexr
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: A malformed OpenEXR header can trigger an invalid memory read (segmentation fault / invalid pointer dereference) in internal_exr_validate_shared_attrs (src/lib/OpenEXRCore/validation.c). During header parsing (internal_exr_parse_header / exr_start_read), memcmp is called on attribute data that has not been properly validated, allowing crafted input to cause a crash/undefined behavior (denial-of-service).
build_sh:
cd $WORK/
CMAKE_SETTINGS=(
  "-D BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF"         # Build static libraries only
  "-D BUILD_TESTING=OFF"             # Or tests
  "-D OPENEXR_INSTALL_EXAMPLES=OFF"  # Or examples
  "-D OPENEXR_LIB_SUFFIX="           # Don't append the version number to library files
)
cmake $SRC/openexr ${CMAKE_SETTINGS[@]}
make -j$(nproc)
INCLUDES=(
  "-I $SRC"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXRCore"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXR"
  "-I $SRC/openexr/src/lib/OpenEXRUtil"
  "-I $WORK/cmake"
)
LIBS=(
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXRUtil/libOpenEXRUtil.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXR/libOpenEXR.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/OpenEXRCore/libOpenEXRCore.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/IlmThread/libIlmThread.a"
  "$WORK/src/lib/Iex/libIex.a"
  "$WORK/_deps/imath-build/src/Imath/libImath*.a"
)
for fuzzer in $SRC/openexr/src/test/OpenEXRFuzzTest/oss-fuzz/*_fuzzer.cc; do
  fuzzer_basename=$(basename -s .cc $fuzzer)
  $CXX $CXXFLAGS -std=c++11 -pthread ${INCLUDES[@]} $fuzzer $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE ${LIBS[@]} -lz     -o $OUT/$fuzzer_basename
done

# Project-specific: openexr
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /work/bin/exrcheck /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/openexr

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y make autoconf automake libtool mercurial git libpcre2-dev zlib1g-dev
RUN git clone https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/openexr openexr
RUN git -C openexr checkout 97e2d2953805dd9f1f6a6cf42d2a31812481d5cc
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/openexr
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: An invalid memory read (segmentation fault) occurs in internal_exr_validate_shared_attrs (src/lib/OpenEXRCore/validation.c). During header parsing (internal_exr_parse_header) the code invokes memcmp on attribute data that can reference out-of-bounds or uninitialized memory for crafted/malformed files, causing a crash. This can be triggered by attacker-supplied inputs and leads to denial-of-service during OpenEXR header validation.

bug_description: An invalid memory read occurs in zend_ast_export_ex (Zend/zend_ast.c): when exporting certain AST node kinds the function performs an 8-byte read from an invalid address, causing an ASAN-detected crash during AST export/compilation. This can be triggered by crafted input and leads to parser/compiler crashes (denial-of-service).

#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -eu
./buildconf --force
./configure \
        --enable-option-checking=fatal \
        --disable-dom \
        --disable-simplexml \
        --disable-xml \
        --disable-xmlreader \
        --disable-xmlwriter \
        --without-pear \
        --enable-exif \
        --disable-phpdbg \
        --disable-cgi
make -j$(nproc)

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in Zend: zend_assign_to_variable (reached via zend_std_write_property) can read a zval/object byte after it has been freed during object property assignment. This use-after-free can lead to crashes, memory corruption, information disclosure, or potentially arbitrary code execution.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -eu
./buildconf --force
./configure \
        --enable-option-checking=fatal \
        --disable-dom \
        --disable-simplexml \
        --disable-xml \
        --disable-xmlreader \
        --disable-xmlwriter \
        --without-pear \
        --enable-exif \
        --disable-phpdbg \
        --disable-cgi
make -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    USE_ZEND_ALLOC=0 /src/php-src/sapi/cli/php /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/php-src

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/php-src
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap use-after-free in Zend/zend_generators.c: zend_generator_search_multi_children_node (reachable via zend_generator_add_child and zend_generator_yield_from) reads memory from a freed generator-related structure during "yield from" operations. This can cause crashes or other undefined behavior and may enable memory-corruption-based exploits when fed crafted generator state.

bug_description: A heap-use-after-free in Zend/zend_execute.c: the function zend_assign_to_string_offset (invoked by the ZEND_ASSIGN_DIM_SPEC_VAR_CV_OP_DATA_CONST_HANDLER VM opcode) can read from memory that has already been freed (freed by zend_array_destroy), leading to crashes, heap corruption and potential memory disclosure or other exploitable behavior.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -eu
./buildconf --force
./configure \
        --enable-option-checking=fatal \
        --disable-dom \
        --disable-simplexml \
        --disable-xml \
        --disable-xmlreader \
        --disable-xmlwriter \
        --without-pear \
        --enable-exif \
        --disable-phpdbg \
        --disable-cgi
make -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    USE_ZEND_ALLOC=0 /src/php-src/sapi/cli/php /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/php-src

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update
RUN apt-get install -y make autoconf automake libtool bison re2c make ca-certificates curl xz-utils dpkg-dev file libc-dev pkg-config libcurl4-openssl-dev libedit-dev libsqlite3-dev libssl-dev zlib1g-dev libxml2-dev
RUN git clone https://github.com/php/php-src.git php-src
RUN git -C php-src checkout efbb2198d4e5a01167d1a8c2937b848f75ac4d19
RUN git clone https://github.com/kkos/oniguruma.git oniguruma
RUN git -C oniguruma checkout d432bc049f5519c4d8f46f54497ef202ccc7406b
RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/php-src
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in Zend hash lookup: zend_hash_find_bucket (Zend/zend_hash.c) performs an out-of-bounds read when resolving attribute/constant values during attribute validation (zend_get_attribute_value / validate_attribute in Zend/zend_attributes.c). patch:
commit f06afc434aec631f18f8da8ffca9a6f0559e1acf
Author: Nikita Popov <nikita.ppv@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Dec 1 11:49:27 2020 +0100

    Don't use scope when validating Attribute
    
    This is not safe to do at this point. Even if we made it safe,
    we'd see inconsistencies due to a partially compiled class.
    
    Fixes oss-fuzz #28129.

diff --git a/Zend/zend_attributes.c b/Zend/zend_attributes.c
index 29a2f4a7325..ae07802b5bc 100644
--- a/Zend/zend_attributes.c
+++ b/Zend/zend_attributes.c
@@ -33,7 +33,10 @@ void validate_attribute(zend_attribute *attr, uint32_t target, zend_class_entry
 	if (attr->argc > 0) {
 		zval flags;
 
-		if (FAILURE == zend_get_attribute_value(&flags, attr, 0, scope)) {
+		/* As this is run in the middle of compilation, fetch the attribute value without
+		 * specifying a scope. The class is not fully linked yet, and we may seen an
+		 * inconsistent state. */
+		if (FAILURE == zend_get_attribute_value(&flags, attr, 0, NULL)) {
 			return;
 		}

bug_description: Stack-use-after-return in PHP's unserialization code (php_var_unserialize_internal / process_nested_data in ext/standard/var_unserializer.re). During deserialization of crafted input, zval_get_type ends up reading a zval that refers to stack memory which has gone out of scope, causing reads of invalid stack memory and leading to crashes or other undefined behavior during unserialize.

        --without-pear \
        --enable-exif \
        --disable-phpdbg \

secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/php-src/sapi/cli/php "$@" /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/php-src

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "${@:2}"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in ext/hash/hash_xxhash.c (PHP_XXH64Update / XXH_INLINE_XXH64_update / XXH_memcpy). When processing an unserialized/updated xxhash context, an unchecked length derived from input is passed to memcpy, causing reads past the 32-byte hash state buffer. This results in heap out-of-bounds reads that can crash the process or lead to memory disclosure.

bug_description: The XXH32 implementation in ext/hash/xxhash (XXH_INLINE_XXH32_update / XXH_memcpy, invoked via PHP_XXH32Update in ext/hash/hash_xxhash.c) can perform a memcpy with overlapping source and destination ranges. This memcpy-param-overlap triggers undefined behavior (MSAN/ASAN unknown reads), potentially causing memory corruption, crashes, and incorrect hashing results.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -eu
./buildconf --force
./configure \
        --enable-option-checking=fatal \
        --disable-dom \
        --disable-simplexml \
        --disable-xml \
        --disable-xmlreader \
        --disable-xmlwriter \
        --without-pear \
        --enable-exif \
        --disable-phpdbg \
        --disable-cgi
make -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/php-src/sapi/cli/php /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/php-src

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: A memcpy-parameter-overlap occurs in the XXH32 update path (XXH_memcpy / XXH_INLINE_XXH32_update called from PHP_XXH32Update in ext/hash/hash_xxhash.c / xxhash.h), causing memcpy to be invoked on overlapping source and destination ranges. This leads to undefined behavior and a heap-buffer-overflow read, resulting in memory corruption and crashes during hash update/unserialize operations (potential denial-of-service or further exploitation).

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in the Zend engine: div_function (Zend/zend_operators.c) may free/destroy the result zval too early during a compound division assignment when the destination is the same as the dividend. If the division operation fails (for example, division by zero), subsequent bytecode handlers (e.g. ZEND_ASSIGN_OP_SPEC_* handlers) can access or add a reference to the already-freed zval (seen in zend_gc_addref), resulting in a heap-use-after-free that can crash the interpreter and may be exploitable.

        --disable-xmlreader \
        --disable-xmlwriter \
        --without-pear \

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in Zend/zend_closures.c: zend_closure_free_storage can end up invoking zend_string_release on a function name that has already been freed, causing subsequent reads of freed heap memory (seen via zend_string_release and zend_objects_store_del). This use-after-free during closure/object destruction can cause crashes, memory corruption and may be exploitable for information disclosure or arbitrary code execution.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -eu
./buildconf --force
./configure \
        --enable-option-checking=fatal \
        --disable-dom \
        --disable-simplexml \
        --disable-xml \
        --disable-xmlreader \
        --disable-xmlwriter \
        --without-pear \
        --enable-exif \
        --disable-phpdbg \
        --disable-cgi
make -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    USE_ZEND_ALLOC=0 /src/php-src/sapi/cli/php /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/php-src

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in the Zend VM: ZEND_FE_FETCH_RW_SPEC_VAR_HANDLER (Zend/zend_vm_execute.h) performs a read from heap memory that has already been freed. The freed region is released via zend_array_destroy (zend_hash.c) during ArrayIterator setup (ext/spl/spl_array.c, zim_ArrayIterator___construct / spl_array_set_array), but later VM bytecode handlers still access the deallocated memory. This use-after-free can cause crashes, memory corruption and may lead to further exploitation such as arbitrary code execution.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -eu
./buildconf --force
./configure \
        --enable-option-checking=fatal \
        --disable-dom \
        --disable-simplexml \
        --disable-xml \
        --disable-xmlreader \
        --disable-xmlwriter \
        --without-pear \
        --enable-exif \
        --disable-phpdbg \
        --disable-cgi
make -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    USE_ZEND_ALLOC=0 /src/php-src/sapi/cli/php /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/php-src

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in the date extension: timelib_error_container_dtor (ext/date/lib/timelib.c) reads a timelib_error_container that has already been freed (freed earlier by update_errors_warnings during DateTime initialization in ext/date/php_date.c). The freed container is later accessed during module/request deactivation (zm_post_zend_deactivate_date / zend_post_deactivate_modules), causing reads of freed heap memory that can lead to crashes, undefined behavior, and potential exploitation.

        --disable-xmlreader \

        --without-pear \

        --disable-phpdbg \
        --disable-cgi
make -j$(nproc)

#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    USE_ZEND_ALLOC=0 /src/php-src/sapi/cli/php /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/php-src

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

bug_description: php_fgetcsv in ext/standard/file.c (invoked via zif_str_getcsv) can compute an incorrect copy length and pass a negative size to memcpy (ASAN negative-size-param). This leads to an invalid memcpy call that can cause memory corruption/heap overflow and crashes when parsing specially crafted CSV input, potentially enabling denial-of-service or other memory-safety issues.

bug_description: Heap-use-after-free in xbuf_format_converter (main/spprintf.c): xbuf_format_converter performs a strlen on a buffer that has already been freed while building formatted strings via zend_vstrpprintf / zend_error_va_list. This read-after-free is reachable from error/convert paths (observed during string-to-long conversion in bitwise AND handling) and can cause process crashes or other memory-corruption issues when triggered.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Minimized build script with only core build commands
set -eu
./buildconf --force
./configure \
        --enable-option-checking=fatal \
        --disable-dom \
        --disable-simplexml \
        --disable-xml \
        --disable-xmlreader \
        --disable-xmlwriter \
        --without-pear \
        --enable-exif \
        --disable-phpdbg \
        --disable-cgi
make -j$(nproc)
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    USE_ZEND_ALLOC=0 /src/php-src/sapi/cli/php /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/php-src

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

RUN apt-get install -y make autoconf automake libtool bison re2c make ca-certificates curl xz-utils dpkg-dev file libc-dev pkg-config libcurl4-openssl-dev libedit-dev libsqlite3-dev libssl-dev zlib1g-dev libxml2-dev
RUN git clone https://github.com/php/php-src.git php-src

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) in PackMachBase::canUnpack (p_mach.cpp) via get_ne32/get_le32 (bele.h). While iterating Mach-O load commands (including fat/32-bit paths), a malformed or too-short command can cause a 4-byte read past the allocated buffer, leading to crashes and possible disclosure of heap memory.

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) in ELF parsing: an 8‑byte read in get_ne64 / BEPolicy::get64 (src/bele.h / src/bele_policy.h), invoked from PackLinuxElf64::PackLinuxElf64help1 (src/p_lx_elf.cpp, seen during PackLinuxElf64ppc construction), can read past the end of an ELF-derived buffer when processing symbol table information. A crafted ELF input can trigger this out‑of‑bounds read, leading to crashes or potential disclosure of memory contents.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash

set -e

sed -i 's/ \&\& __clang_major__ < 15//m' /src/upx/src/util/util.cpp
mkdir -p build/debug
cd build/debug
cmake ../..
make upx
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/upx/build/debug/upx /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/upx

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y make autoconf automake libtool cmake

RUN git clone https://github.com/upx/upx.git upx
RUN git -C upx checkout 24b4ec42e701814b5bf55685f6fbc81e25f1044c
RUN git clone https://github.com/MykolaMykhno/upx-testsuite-v3.git testsuite3
RUN git -C testsuite3 checkout 30bd1b1f36fb29128d52cab4be2c02e3dcef40e4
RUN git clone https://github.com/upx/upx-testsuite-v2.git testsuite2
RUN git -C testsuite2 checkout 294c60f094d10b9b729d7a7188dbea6802716f5b
RUN git clone https://github.com/upx/upx-testsuite.git testsuite
RUN git -C testsuite checkout 28ac37ca370045a75faa3714380cd2182174eb52

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/upx
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: A bounds-checking error in PackUnix::find_overlay_offset (src/p_unix.cpp) allows a 4-byte read past the end of the input buffer via the little-endian 32-bit reader (get_le32 / N_BELE_RTP::LEPolicy::get32). Malformed input can trigger a heap-buffer-overflow read during unpack detection (PackUnix::canUnpack), leading to crashes (DoS) and potential information disclosure.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash

set -e

sed -i 's/ \&\& __clang_major__ < 15//m' /src/upx/src/util/util.cpp
mkdir -p build/debug
cd build/debug
cmake ../..
make upx

-    if (l < 0 || l + 4 > bufsize)
+    if (l < 0 || i + l + 4 > bufsize)
         throwCantUnpack("file corrupted");
     overlay_offset = get_te32(buf + i + l);
     if ((off_t)overlay_offset >= file_size)

bug_description: Stack-buffer-overflow in PackLinuxElf64::unpack (src/p_lx_elf.cpp): when locating a b_info structure inside a local peek buffer the code computes an offset and performs a 4-byte read (via get_le32/get_ne32) without ensuring the offset + sizeof(b_info) fits inside the buffer. A crafted packed ELF can trigger an out‑of‑bounds read on the stack, causing crashes and potential information disclosure or other undefined behavior.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash

set -e

sed -i 's/ \&\& __clang_major__ < 15//m' /src/upx/src/util/util.cpp
mkdir -p build/debug
cd build/debug
cmake ../..
make upx
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/upx/build/debug/upx -t /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/upx

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi

RUN git clone https://github.com/upx/upx-testsuite-v2.git testsuite2
RUN git -C testsuite2 checkout a1b4a7c219d3f13008036a0d3376f807c027d2ea
RUN git clone https://github.com/upx/upx-testsuite.git testsuite
RUN git -C testsuite checkout 844642407f2894ea2d5fc0912b02aa5abb12d0c6

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/upx
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow in PackLinuxElf64::unpack (p_lx_elf.cpp). While unpacking ELF data, N_BELE_RTP::LEPolicy::get64 / get_le64 (bele_policy.h / bele.h) can read an 8-byte little-endian value past the end of a MemBuffer allocation, due to insufficient bounds validation of ELF header/segment data. This out-of-bounds read can cause crashes and may lead to information disclosure when processing malformed ELF files.

set -e

sed -i 's/ \&\& __clang_major__ < 15//m' /src/upx/src/util/util.cpp
mkdir -p build/debug
cd build/debug
cmake ../..
make upx

bug_description: Heap-based buffer overflow (out-of-bounds read) in PackLinuxElf64::elf_lookup (p_lx_elf.cpp). While processing crafted/malformed ELF string table data, strcmp can read past the end of the mapped file buffer, causing a one-byte heap read beyond the allocation. This can crash UPX or lead to memory disclosure when handling malicious ELF files.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash

set -e

sed -i 's/ \&\& __clang_major__ < 15//m' /src/upx/src/util/util.cpp
mkdir -p build/debug
cd build/debug
cmake ../..
make upx
secb_sh:
#!/bin/bash

build() {
    echo "BUILDING THE PROJECT..."

    # Handle git sub-modules
    if [[ -f .gitmodules || -f .gitmodule ]]; then
        echo "Detected git sub-modules - initialising/updating..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive
    else
        echo "No git sub-modules found - skipping update."
    fi

    # Check for repo_changes.diff and apply if it exists and hasn't been applied yet
    if [[ -f /testcase/repo_changes.diff ]]; then
        # Check if the patch has already been applied to avoid re-applying
        if ! git apply --check /testcase/repo_changes.diff &>/dev/null; then
            echo "Repository changes already applied or cannot be applied cleanly. Proceeding with build."
        else
            echo "Applying repository changes from repo_changes.diff..."
            git apply /testcase/repo_changes.diff || echo "Warning: Could not apply repo_changes.diff cleanly. Proceeding anyway."
        fi
    fi

    # stdout: /dev/null
    # stderr: grep filters out "warning:" and lets everything else through
    if /usr/local/bin/compile \
         1>/dev/null \
         2> >(grep -Fv --line-buffered -e "warning:" -e "SyntaxWarning:" -e "WARNING:" >&2); then
        echo "BUILD COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "BUILD FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}

repro() {
    /src/upx/build/debug/upx -l /testcase/poc
}

patch() {
    echo "PATCHING THE PROJECT..."
    cd /src/upx

    if [[ ! -f /testcase/model_patch.diff ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: /testcase/model_patch.diff not found. Please save the file before patching."
        exit 1
    fi

    if git apply /testcase/model_patch.diff; then
        echo "PATCH APPLIED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    else
        echo "PATCH APPLICATION FAILED!"
        exit 1
    fi
}


if [ "$#" -ge 1 ]; then
    command="$1"

    case "$command" in
        build)
            build "$@"
            ;;
        repro)
            repro "$@"
            ;;
        patch)
            patch "$@"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
else
    echo "Usage: secb [build|repro|patch]"
    exit 1
fi
dockerfile:
FROM hwiwonlee/secb.base:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y make autoconf automake libtool cmake

RUN git clone https://github.com/upx/upx.git upx
RUN git -C upx checkout ff5a4eeb9673da0c24b45fb163a0f84bfba1ea05
RUN git clone https://github.com/MykolaMykhno/upx-testsuite-v3.git testsuite3
RUN git -C testsuite3 checkout 30bd1b1f36fb29128d52cab4be2c02e3dcef40e4
RUN git clone https://github.com/upx/upx-testsuite-v2.git testsuite2
RUN git -C testsuite2 checkout a1b4a7c219d3f13008036a0d3376f807c027d2ea
RUN git clone https://github.com/upx/upx-testsuite.git testsuite
RUN git -C testsuite checkout 844642407f2894ea2d5fc0912b02aa5abb12d0c6

RUN mkdir -p /testcase

WORKDIR $SRC/upx
COPY build.sh $SRC/
COPY secb /usr/local/bin/secb
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/secb

ENV FUZZING_LANGUAGE=c

bug_description: PackLinuxElf32::un_asl_dynsym in p_lx_elf.cpp can dereference an invalid/null dynamic-string pointer while processing ELF dynamic symbols. The bug leads to adjABS calling strcmp on an invalid dynstr-derived pointer, producing an invalid read/segmentation fault when unpacking specially crafted ELF inputs. This results in a crash (denial-of-service) when handling malformed/partial ELF files.
build_sh:
#!/bin/bash

set -e

sed -i 's/ \&\& __clang_major__ < 15//m' /src/upx/src/util/util.cpp
mkdir -p build/debug
cd build/debug
cmake ../..
make upx

bug_description: Heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) in PackLinuxElf32::elf_lookup (src/p_lx_elf.cpp). While walking ELF DT_HASH chains to resolve symbols the code can index past the end of the chains table and invoke a 32-bit read (via the get32/get_le32 path), causing a 4-byte heap OOB read. A malformed or malicious DT_HASH can therefore trigger crashes and potential information disclosure.
